description: Add /study research mode for 2-week human pilot study — consent flow, stress session (Block A), food-emotion session (Block B), and admin dashboard with CSV export for paper analysis

userStories.title: Add study_participants and study_sessions database tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables to store anonymous participant consent and EEG session data for the pilot study.
acceptanceCriteria: Add study_participants table: id (serial pk), participant_code (varchar unique), age (int), diet_type (varchar), has_apple_watch (boolean), consent_text (text), consent_timestamp (timestamp), created_at (timestamp default now())

Add study_sessions table: id (serial pk), participant_code (varchar), block_type (varchar: stress|food|sleep), pre_eeg_json (jsonb), post_eeg_json (jsonb), eeg_features_json (jsonb), survey_json (jsonb), intervention_triggered (boolean default false), created_at (timestamp default now())
Drizzle migration generated and runs successfully with drizzle-kit push
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add study API routes to Express server
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST API endpoints to handle participant consent, session creation, session completion, and admin data access.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/study/consent — accepts { participant_code, age, diet_type, has_apple_watch, consent_text }, saves to study_participants, returns { success: true, participant_code }
POST /api/study/session/start — accepts { participant_code, block_type }, creates pending study_sessions record, returns { session_id }
POST /api/study/session/complete — accepts { session_id, pre_eeg_json, post_eeg_json, eeg_features_json, survey_json, intervention_triggered }, updates session record, returns { success: true }
GET /api/study/admin/participants — returns all study_participants rows (requires auth middleware)
GET /api/study/admin/sessions — returns all study_sessions rows joined with participant data (requires auth middleware)

GET /api/study/admin/export-csv — returns CSV file with headers: participant_code, block_type, age, diet_type, has_apple_watch, pre_alpha, pre_beta, pre_theta, pre_delta, pre_gamma, post_alpha, post_beta, post_theta, post_delta, post_gamma, intervention_triggered, and all survey_json numeric fields flattened
All routes return 400 for missing required fields, 401 for unauthorized admin routes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build /study landing page
userStories.description: As a potential participant, I want to understand the study and click to join from a single shareable page.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study route

Page displays: study title 'Neural Dream Workshop Pilot Study', what participants do (wear Muse 2, complete stress and food sessions), time required (25-40 min total), that participation is anonymous and voluntary
Join the Study button navigates to /study/consent
Page is mobile-responsive with readable text on small screens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /study/consent page with digital consent form
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to read the informed consent text, check agreement, enter my participant code, and proceed.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study/consent
Displays full consent text: what data is collected (EEG features and self-report surveys), how it is used (academic research paper), that participation is voluntary, right to withdraw at any time
Checkbox input: 'I have read the above and agree to participate voluntarily'
Text input: 'Enter your participant code (e.g. P001)' with validation (must match pattern P + 3 digits)
Continue button is disabled until both checkbox is checked and participant code is entered
On submit: POST to /api/study/consent with consent_text snapshot and participant_code
On success: navigate to /study/profile passing participant_code in state or query param
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /study/profile page
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to enter basic demographic info and choose which session block to start.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study/profile
Age number input (18-99 range)
Diet type dropdown: Omnivore, Vegetarian, Vegan, Other
Apple Watch toggle: Yes / No
Start Stress Session button navigates to /study/session/stress
Start Food Session button navigates to /study/session/food
Profile data submitted via PUT to /api/study/consent or PATCH endpoint before navigating
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /study/session/stress page — Block A (stress at work)
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to complete the 25-minute stress session: baseline EEG, work task with live stress monitoring, breathing intervention, post-EEG, and survey.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study/session/stress

Step 1 Baseline: 5-minute countdown timer, instruction text 'Sit still with eyes closed', calls /api/analyze-eeg or /api/simulate-eeg every 4 seconds to collect EEG data, stores averaged features as pre_eeg_json
Step 2 Work Task: 15-minute countdown timer, text 'Continue your normal work with headband on', displays live stress level as a colored progress bar (green < 0.4, yellow 0.4-0.65, red > 0.65)

Step 3 Intervention: triggers automatically when stress_score > 0.65 OR at 15-minute mark; shows Box Breathing or Physio Sigh component (reuse existing breathing exercise UI); sets intervention_triggered = true
Step 4 Post-EEG: 5-minute countdown timer, collects EEG data, stores as post_eeg_json
Step 5 Survey: three sliders (1-10): 'How stressed did you feel during the session?', 'Did the breathing exercise help?', 'How do you feel right now?'; stores as survey_json
Progress stepper shows current step (1 of 5) at top of page
On completion: POST to /api/study/session/complete, navigate to /study/complete
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /study/session/food page — Block B (food + emotional state)
userStories.description: As a participant, I want to complete the food-emotion session: pre-meal EEG baseline, eat normally, post-meal EEG, and food survey.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study/session/food
Step 1 Pre-meal survey: sliders (1-10) for 'How hungry are you?' and 'What is your current mood?'
Step 2 Pre-meal EEG: 5-minute countdown timer with Muse on, collects EEG data, stores as pre_eeg_json
Step 3 Eat: instruction screen 'Remove the headband and eat your normal meal. Tap the button below when you finish eating.' — button says 'I finished eating', no auto-timer
Step 4 Wait: 5-minute countdown timer after finishing meal, text 'Please wait 5 minutes before putting the headband back on'
Step 5 Post-meal EEG: 10-minute countdown timer with Muse on, collects EEG data, stores as post_eeg_json

Step 6 Post-meal survey: text input 'What did you eat? (brief description)', sliders (1-10) for 'How healthy was this meal?', 'Energy level now?', 'Mood now?', 'Do you feel satisfied?'; stores as survey_json
Progress stepper shows current step at top of page
On completion: POST to /api/study/session/complete, navigate to /study/complete
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /study/complete confirmation page
userStories.description: As a participant, I want confirmation that my session was saved and clear next steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study/complete
Shows 'Session complete — thank you!' with participant code displayed
If only stress session done: shows 'Next: complete the Food session to finish your participation' with button to /study/session/food
If only food session done: shows 'Next: complete the Stress session' with button to /study/session/stress
If both sessions done: shows 'You have completed both sessions. Your data helps advance brain science. Thank you!' with no further CTAs
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /study/admin dashboard with CSV export
userStories.description: As Sravya, I want to see all participant data and session results, and export everything as a CSV file for Python analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Page exists at /study/admin, redirects to login if not authenticated
Summary stats at top: total participants, stress sessions completed, food sessions completed
Table with columns: participant_code, age, diet_type, has_apple_watch, sessions_completed count, last session date
Clicking a row expands to show session details: block_type, intervention_triggered, survey scores as key-value pairs
Export CSV button calls GET /api/study/admin/export-csv and triggers file download named study-data-YYYY-MM-DD.csv
CSV includes all numeric EEG band powers (pre and post) and all survey numeric values as separate columns
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
